headline: Cooking Oil Subsidy Costs RM4.1B as Smuggling Persists
context: The government spent RM4.1 billion subsidising cooking oil in 2025 while KPDN estimated that 28% of subsidised palm olein was smuggled to Thailand and Indonesia where prices are 40% higher.

cards.big: RM4.1 billion on cooking oil subsidies — but 28% gets smuggled across borders, costing RM1.15 billion in leakage
cards.sub: Subsidised at RM2.50 per kg in Malaysia, same oil sells for RM4.80 in Thailand — arbitrage is irresistible

cards.big: Malaysia subsidises cooking oil at world's lowest price — but plantation companies sell CPO at world market rates
cards.sub: Subsidy effectively transfers RM4.1 billion from government to plantation companies who face no price cap on CPO

cards.big: B40 households consume 4.2 kg of cooking oil monthly — subsidy saves them RM120 annually per family
cards.sub: T20 restaurants and food manufacturers consume 62% of subsidised cooking oil volume by weight

cards.big: KPDN deployed 1,200 enforcement officers to monitor 48,000 retail outlets — 1 officer per 40 shops
cards.sub: Only 342 smuggling prosecutions in 2025 despite satellite data showing 2,800 suspicious cross-border movements

cards.big: Subsidising cooking oil for everyone including restaurants and smugglers is the most expensive way to help the people who need it least
Should cooking oil subsidies shift to direct cash transfers of RM150 monthly to verified B40 households only?
